items.reason: site/index.html and site/docs/index.html render proper HTML with navigation/styling. check_spec_conformance confirms no href to .md/.yaml/.yml under site/. OpenAPI has Redoc pages (site/openapi/hosted-json.html, oneroster-core.html, qti-core.html).

site/index.html has explicit 'Platform Provides' and 'App Developer Owns' sections with concrete bullets naming OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, Open Badges/CLR; app-owner items include 'pedagogy', 'UI quality', and 'scope minimization'.

Each generator (e.g., scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py) reads a `dictionary/*.v1.json` and emits SQL comments, OpenAPI JSON, Markdown, and HTML in one run. scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies the artifacts stay synchronized with the source.

All seven Lead specs are accounted for: dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json, qti-core.v1.json, case-core.v1.json, caliper-core.v1.json, and integration-governance-core.v1.json (covering LTI, Security Framework, Data Privacy). Each contains many objects and fields, not placeholders.

Dictionaries declare `source_standard` at the top level and lead-spec-accounting.md lists deferred areas with business reasons (e.g., 'requires hosted auth, certification testing, pagination'), but per-field `sourceStandard` references and a comprehensive field-level unsupported ledger are not present — OneRoster broader 1.2 (demographics, agents, resources, score scales) is openly partial. Backlog item 10 tracks the gap.

Sampled fields show plain-language descriptions a non-engineer can read: e.g., person.display_name = 'The name shown in user interfaces.', organization = 'A district, school, department, program, or other education organization.'. scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces description presence on every field.

demo/server.js exposes /users/{sourcedId} that returns OneRoster-shaped JSON ({user: {sourcedId, status, roles, ...}}); demo/test.js asserts user.sourcedId === 'USER-ADA' and confirms `id` is not exposed. VERIFY.md runs `cd demo && npm run reset-db && npm test`.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py issues live calls against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results with the publishable key. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms verified-live status on 2026-04-26 with expected counts.

site/index.html includes a browser SQL console (sql.js loading demo/schema.sql + seed.sql) with sample queries, AND a copy-pasteable curl block against the live Supabase REST URL with the publishable key. Both execute against real data, not screenshots.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md has 10 numbered decisions covering Person identity, Class/course/context, Roles, Enrollment vs. membership, Results/scores, Standards alignment, Identifiers, Time/session, Content/resource, and Tenancy. Each entry names specs, the conflict, the choice, a per-spec mapping table, and an explicit tradeoff paragraph.

Machine-readable trace block in standards-overlap-decisions.md lists `produces_fields` per decision_id with paths like `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.display_name`, and dictionary fields carry a `decision_id` attribute. check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces both directions.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql adds tenant_id columns to all fact tables, calls `enable row level security` and `force row level security`, and creates policies with predicates like `tenant_id = coalesce(nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id','')::uuid, ...)` — never `using (true)`. pg_policies.snapshot.json mirrors the same predicates. tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py creates two JWTs with different tenant_id claims and verifies cross-tenant reads are blocked through the live REST URL; VERIFY.md runs it each iteration.

dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json defines `oauth_scope_policies` mapping scopes like `platform.roster.users.directory:read` to specific field refs with privacy_ceiling=directory, and `https://purl.imsglobal.org/spec/lti-ags/scope/result.readonly` to result fields with privacy_ceiling=education_record. Generated OpenAPI emits a `platformOAuth` security scheme and per-field `x-oauthScopes`.

Every sampled field across oneroster-core, caliper-core, case-core, qti-core, and integration-governance-core dictionaries carries a non-empty `privacy_class` drawn from a documented vocabulary (operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, system, public). check_dictionary_artifacts.py requires `privacy_class` in REQUIRED_FIELD_KEYS.

Only tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py uses SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (with inline comment 'Admin operation: tenant_rls_test_auth_fixture_setup'), and docs/admin-operations.md lists that exact operation with caller, why_user_jwt_insufficient, and last_reviewed 2026-04-26 (within 30 days of the 2026-04-24 research date). The Edge Function uses ANON_KEY/PUBLISHABLE_KEY, not service role.

No `audit_log` table exists in supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql, and no Postgres trigger or Edge Function wrapper records reads of directory_pii/education_record fields with client_id, scope, purpose, field_accessed, tenant_id, or timestamp. Backlog item 18 acknowledges this is 'Not done'.

docs/spec-gap-backlog.md contains a 'Rubric Trace For Open Items' table that maps rubric IDs (lead_spec_full_accounting, tenant_isolation_enforced, oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields, audit_log_for_sensitive_reads, edge_functions_for_non_crud_endpoints, edge_functions_propagate_user_jwt, rls_enabled_on_referenced_tables) to specific backlog work items with status, target slice, and owner notes.

docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md has the four required columns (Structured source, Generated artifacts, Runnable slice, Unsupported ledger) and populates them honestly per spec — e.g., QTI shows 'No' for Runnable slice rather than deflecting to backlog.

supabase/functions/gradebook-bulk-submit/{index.ts,deno.json} implements one non-CRUD endpoint (gradebook bulk submit) that verifies the JWT and forwards Authorization to the Supabase client so RLS applies; site/index.html documents the live function URL with a curl example. However LTI 1.3 launch handler, Caliper event ingestion, and OAuth token exchange Edge Functions do not exist.

supabase/functions/gradebook-bulk-submit/index.ts constructs the Supabase client with `global.headers.Authorization = authorization` from `req.headers.get('Authorization')` and never references SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. docs/admin-operations.md exists as the allowlist; no service-role usage in any Edge Function to allowlist.

supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json shows every public table (organizations, people, academic_sessions, courses, classes, enrollments, line_items, results, source_identifiers) with rowsecurity=true, forceRowSecurity=true, and non-trivial USING clauses referencing `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'`. generatedAt 2026-04-26T10:38:47Z aligns with the current research date. Other dictionary specs (CASE/QTI/Caliper/LTI) are not yet runtime-backed.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py hits /rest/v1/people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co with the publishable key and is run in VERIFY.md. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms migrations/seed/smoke loaded successfully and REST returns ok:true.

site/index.html embeds a copy-pasteable curl with the public publishable key `sb_publishable_DaJsnILCWdUIjl4cCaL3Jw_qLy8BPXK` against /rest/v1/people; no env vars or non-public keys required. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md repeats the same curl for reviewers.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. evaluate_platform.py writes a report containing per-rubric `items` array (each with id, status, reason, evidence_paths) plus `counts: {pass, partial, fail, blocked, total}` and `done`.

scripts/check_spec_conformance.py runs in VERIFY.md and writes site/api/spec-conformance.json with the explicit comment 'ADVISORY ONLY... The Codex loop now gates publish on `scripts/evaluate_platform.py`'. scripts/codex_loop.py's maybe_publish() gates on `judge_result.publish_recommendation` and rubric progress (`rubric_progressed(before_eval, after_eval)`), not on numeric score; evaluate_platform.py loads the advisory report into the LLM context as one signal.

scripts/codex_loop.py defines read_overrides() reading docs/loop-overrides.md, overrides_paused() short-circuits BEFORE running an iteration when a line equals literal `PAUSE` (excluding HTML-commented examples), and build_prompt() injects the cleaned contents under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section. The file is referenced from site/docs/index.html (loop-overrides.html), confirming presence.

scripts/codex_loop.py checks `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): print('Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop.'); return 0`, where `done` comes from the LLM evaluator's rubric tally (true iff all pass or every non-pass is blocked with prerequisites). Hard caps remain via --max-iterations, --stall-limit, and DEFAULT_COST_CEILING_USD.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
